name: Spaghetti Sauce Without Meat
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 9 pints
Procedure:
1. Do not increase the proportion of onions, peppers, or mushrooms.
2. Wash tomatoes and dip in boiling water for 30 to 60 seconds or until skins split. Dip in cold water and slip off skins. Remove cores and quarter tomatoes.
3. Boil tomatoes in a large saucepan for 20 minutes, uncovered.
4. Put through food mill or sieve.
5. Saute onions, garlic, celery, or peppers, and mushrooms (if desired) in vegetable oil until tender.
6. Combine sauteed vegetables and tomatoes and add the remainder of spices, salt, and sugar. Bring to a boil.
7. Simmer uncovered until thick enough for serving. The initial volume will have been reduced by nearly one-half. Stir frequently to avoid burning.
8. Fill jars, leaving 1-inch headspace.
9. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1 or Table 2, depending on the method of canning used.
Table 1: Recommended process time for Spaghetti Sauce Without Meat in a dial-gauge pressure canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Pints, Quarts.
Process Time: 20 minutes for Pints, 25 minutes for Quarts.
Canner Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb.
Table 2: Recommended process time for Spaghetti Sauce Without Meat in a weighted-gauge pressure canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Pints, Quarts.
Process Time: 20 minutes for Pints, 25 minutes for Quarts.
Canner Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb.
Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb.
